blurb: The Royal United Services Institute (RUSI) is the world's oldest defence and security think tank; its 'Open Source Intelligence, AI and Disruptive Technologies' research stream covers AI's national-security, governance and command implications.

hiring.note: Official RUSI job opportunities page verified; page says current opportunities appear below and none were listed at fetch time.

publications.title: Gatekeeping the Frontier: When AI Access Becomes a National Security Concern
publications.summary: Long-read RUSI commentary on the export-controls directive limiting access to Anthropic's latest AI model, examining when restricting access to frontier models becomes a national-security question rather than a purely commercial one.

publications.title: When the Algorithm Finds You First: A Case for Social Media Age Restrictions
publications.summary: Argues that recommender algorithms can funnel young people toward extreme and violent content, making the case for social-media age restrictions as a counter-radicalisation measure.

publications.title: The Hour That Worked: What Midnight Hammer Teaches About AI-Era Command
publications.summary: Uses two US operations against Iran nine months apart to draw lessons for NATO on command-and-control in an AI-era military environment.

publications.title: The Case for Banning Superintelligent AI, Before It's Too Late
publications.summary: Guest commentary arguing that Britain's AI sovereignty depends on prohibiting superintelligence, framing a ban on superintelligent AI as a national-security and governance imperative.

notes: Harvested 2026-06-27 via OpenClaw headless browser (Cloudflare-gated to web_fetch/curl). Source: RUSI 'Open Source Intelligence, AI and Disruptive Technologies' topic page → individual commentary pages. Dates taken from the visible byline date on each article (e.g. '22 June 2026'); the page <time> datetime attribute carried an unreliable ingestion timestamp and was NOT used. All four items are AI/national-security commentary; bylines first-party. RUSI's main /news/publications search index was reachable but its dated article cards render via JS pagination, so I used the topic page's surfaced commentary links instead.
